info.title: Nexus API
info.description:
Get live event data with accurate match timing estimates. Visit [frc.nexus/api](https://frc.nexus/api) to get your API key or to register your webhook URLs.

**Availability**
Live event status is only available for events that are using Nexus to manage queuing. Match status and times will not be accurate for events not using Nexus for queuing.

**Timestamps**
All timestamps are represented using [Unix time in milliseconds](https://currentmillis.com). Most timestamps are computed dynamically and could change every time you receive a new status.

**Attribution**
Include a link back to [frc.nexus](https://frc.nexus) in any projects in which you utilize Nexus data.

parameters.name: eventKey
parameters.description: The event you want to fetch the status of.

realEvent.summary: The year & event code of a real event. Get event codes from frc.events or thebluealliance.com.

demoEvent.summary: Your demo event key for testing. Get your demo event key from frc.nexus/api.

401.description: Missing API key. Get your API key from [frc.nexus/api](https://frc.nexus/api) and include it in the `Nexus-Api-Key` header.

403.description: Invalid API key. Ensure the key you're using matches your key at [frc.nexus/api](https://frc.nexus/api). If abuse was detected, your API key may have been disabled. Email contact@frc.nexus for support.

500.description: An unknown server error occurred. Try again later or email contact@frc.nexus for support.

x-codeSamples.source:
import datetime
import requests
import sys

event_key = "YOUR_eventKey_PARAMETER"
url = "https://frc.nexus/api/v1/event/" + event_key

headers = {"Nexus-Api-Key": "YOUR_API_KEY_HERE"}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)

if not response.ok:
  error_message = response.text
  print("Error getting live event status: {}".format(error_message))
  sys.exit()

data = response.json()
print("Successfully got live event status")
print(data)

# Get information about a specific team's next match.
my_team_number = "100"
my_matches = filter(lambda m: my_team_number in m.get('redTeams', []) + m.get('blueTeams', []), data['matches'])
my_next_match = next(filter(lambda m: not m['status'] == 'On field', my_matches), None)

if my_next_match:
  print("Team {}'s next match is {} ({})!".format(my_team_number, my_next_match['label'], my_next_match['status']))

  alliance_color = 'red' if my_team_number in my_next_match.get('redTeams', []) else 'blue'
  print("Put on the {} bumpers".format(alliance_color))

  estimated_queue_time = my_next_match['times'].get('estimatedQueueTime', None)
  if estimated_queue_time:
    print("We will be queued at ~{}".format(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(estimated_queue_time / 1000)))
else:
  print("Team {} doesn't have any future matches scheduled yet".format(my_team_number))

# Get announcements and parts requests.
for announcement in data['announcements']:
  print("Event announcement: {}".format(announcement['announcement']))

for parts_request in data['partsRequests']:
  print("Parts request for team {}: {}".format(parts_request['requestedByTeam'], parts_request['parts']))

requestBody.description:
Register your webhook at [frc.nexus/api](https://frc.nexus/api).

A POST request is made to each webhook when any of the following happens: - a match status is updated - a break time is updated - a team is added to a playoff alliance - an announcement is posted or removed - a parts request is posted or removed Note that some of these events frequently happen multiple times within a short period of time. Build your application to handle repeated requests and ensure you always use the data with the most recent `dataAsOfTime`.

parameters.name: Nexus-Token
parameters.description: The token from [frc.nexus/api](https://frc.nexus/api) that can be used to verify that Nexus is the caller of your webhook.

200.description: Return a 200 status to indicate that the data was received successfully.

default.description:
All other response codes are considered errors and do not result in retries.

Webhooks that consistently fail to return a 200 status will be automatically disabled.

requestBody.description:
Register your webhook for a specific team number at [frc.nexus/api](https://frc.nexus/api).

A POST request is made to each webhook when the match status is updated for a match which includes the specified team number. Multiple status updates could happen quickly so ensure you always use the data with the most recent `dataAsOfTime`.

parameters.name: Nexus-Token
parameters.description: The token from [frc.nexus/api](https://frc.nexus/api) that can be used to verify that Nexus is the caller of your webhook.

200.description: Return a 200 status to indicate that the data was received successfully.

default.description:
All other response codes are considered errors and do not result in retries.

Webhooks that consistently fail to return a 200 status will be automatically disabled.

inspected.description: Whether the team has passed an initial, complete inspection.

status.description: The current inspection status of the team. Not set for demo events. This value is cached and may be a couple minutes out of date.

queuePosition.description: The current position of this team in the inspection queue. Not set for demo events. This value is cached and may be a couple minutes out of date.

EventSummary.description: High level details of an event that is registered for Nexus. Note: being registered for Nexus does not guarantee the event will use any particular feature of Nexus, if at all. Events will not be listed after they have concluded.

start.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this event is scheduled to start.

end.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this event is scheduled to end.

EventKey.description: The unique identifier of the event. For official events, the event key will match the event code as displayed on [frc.events](http://frc.events). Offseason events may use event keys derived from either [frc.events](http://frc.events) or [thebluealliance.com](https://thebluealliance.com), or will be arbitrary. Examples: `2024casf`, `demo1234`

PitAddress.description: A pit address is an arbitrary string usually in the form of letter + number. They are sometimes useful for finding where a team's pit is located within a venue. Examples: `A1`, `C12`

x.description: The width of the map. 10 units is approximately 1 foot.

y.description: The height of the map. 10 units is approximately 1 foot.

team.description: The team number assigned to this pit, if any.

areas.description: Notable areas in or near the pit area, such as `Pit admin`.

label.description: The label of this area. Examples: `Pit admin`, `Inspection`, `Spare parts`, `EMT`, `Radio config`, `Concessions`, `Machine shop`, `Restrooms`
The text of this label. Examples: `Field`, `Practice field`, `Main gym`, `Auxiliary pits`

arrows.description: Arrows that can indicate entrances, exits, and paths.

type.description: Whether this arrow points in a single direction or in both directions. At an angle of 0°, a single arrow points up and a double arrow points up & down.

walls.description: Walls that may surround or divide pit areas. May also be used to indicate areas that are inaccessible by teams.

position.description: The position of the center of this element.

x.description: The position of the center of this element on the horizontal axis. 10 units is approximately 1 foot.

y.description: The position of the center of this element on the vertical axis. 10 units is approximately 1 foot.

x.description: The width of this element. 10 units is approximately 1 foot.

y.description: The height of this element. 10 units is approximately 1 foot.

angle.description: The angle in degrees that this element is rotated about its center.

dataAsOfTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this event status snapshot was generated by Nexus.

nowQueuing.description: The human-friendly label for the latest match that is queuing. Null if the event is not currently queuing a match. Examples: `Practice 1`, `Qualification 24`, `Qualification 24 Replay`, `Playoff 8`, `Final 1`

matches.description: All scheduled matches for this event, sorted by play order (practice matches first, then qualifications, then playoffs). This array is empty if the event has not yet scheduled any matches. This array can include scheduled match replays which are indicated by the `replayOf` property.

announcements.description: Current announcements that are posted for this event. This array is empty if there are no current announcements.

partsRequests.description: Current parts requests that are posted for this event. This array is empty if there are no current parts requests.

dataAsOfTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this event status snapshot was generated by Nexus.

label.description: The human-friendly label for a match. Examples: `Practice 1`, `Qualification 24`, `Qualification 24 Replay`, `Playoff 8`, `Final 1`

status.description: The current queuing status of this match. Typically, matches transition from `Queuing soon` -> `Now queuing` -> `On deck` -> `On field`. However, any state transition is possible and some events skip the `Now queuing` state entirely.

times.description: Timestamps for key match status changes based on lead queuer input.

scheduledStartTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this match was originally scheduled to start. Not set for playoffs and whenever scheduled match times are not available.

estimatedQueueTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this match is estimated to be `Now queuing`. If the status of this match is not `Queuing soon`, then this timestamp is equivalent to `actualQueueTime`.

estimatedOnDeckTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this match is estimated to be `On deck`. If the status of this match is `On deck` or `On field`, then this timestamp is equivalent to `actualOnDeckTime`.

estimatedOnFieldTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this match is estimated to be `On field`. If the status of this match is `On field`, then this timestamp is equivalent to `actualOnFieldTime`.

estimatedStartTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this match is estimated to start ("3 - 2 - 1 - Go!").

actualQueueTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which the status of this match was set to `Now queuing`. If the status of this match is `Queuing soon`, then this is null.

actualOnDeckTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which the status of this match was set to `On deck`. If the status of this match is `Queuing soon` or `Now queuing`, then this is null.

actualOnFieldTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which the status of this match was set to `On field`. If the status of this match is not `On field`, then this is null.

breakAfter.description: The label for a break that begins after this match is played. Null if there is no break after this match.

replayOf.description: The match this match is a replay of. Null if this match is not a replay

Teams.description: An array of team numbers in station number order. A team number is null if there is no team assigned to that station (applicable to practice matches). The entire array is null if the alliance is undecided (applicable to playoffs). There can be up to 4 teams in the case of a backup team in playoffs.

id.description: A unique identifier for this announcement.

postedTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this announcement was posted.

id.description: A unique identifier for this parts request.

requestedByTeam.description: The team number requesting the parts.

postedTime.description: The timestamp (Unix time in milliseconds) at which this parts request was posted.

PitAddressesEmpty.summary: Event with no pit addresses assigned

InspectionStatus.summary: Inspections status for a small event

InspectionStatusDemo.summary: Inspections status for a demo event

EventStatusMidPractice.summary: Mid-practice matches with announcement & parts request

announcements.announcement: A replay of Qualification 2 will be the first match played after lunch

MatchStatusPractice.summary: Now queuing notification for a practice match

MatchStatusQualification.summary: On deck notification for a qualification match

MatchStatusReplay.summary: On deck notification for a qualification match replay

MatchStatusPlayoff.summary: On field notification for a playoff match

PitMapWithAngles.summary: A complex pit map with many elements at an angle.

PitMapSimple.summary: A simple pit map with straight rows of pits.
